description_long: This object is a lush cluster of flowering plants featuring several large, peach-orange blossoms rising above a dense base of green, grass-like foliage. The flowers have multiple broad petals arranged in layered whorls, giving them a full and rounded appearance. Each blossom is supported by a slender green stem, with some flowers positioned higher than others, creating a natural, slightly asymmetrical bouquet. The leaves are long, narrow, and pointed, radiating outward from the base and interspersed among the stems. The petals exhibit a soft, matte texture, while the leaves and stems appear slightly glossier and more rigid. This plant would be commonly found in urban parks, landscaped medians, or decorative garden beds.
description: A cluster of peach-orange flowers with layered petals and dense, grass-like green foliage, supported by slender stems. The arrangement is lush and natural, suitable for urban landscaping.
description_view_0: Three prominent peach-orange flowers are visible, with dense green foliage at the base and stems rising upward.
description_view_1: The central flower is most prominent, surrounded by several others, with foliage radiating outward in all directions.
description_view_2: Two large flowers are clearly visible, with the foliage appearing more to the right and the stems slightly angled.
description_view_3: Four flowers are visible from the side, with the tallest flower at the center and foliage spreading widely at the base.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled configuration, with multiple overlapping layers radiating symmetrically around the center of each flower, creating a full, rounded bloom typical of ornamental garden flowers.
